pi_items.source: Avare app source code and Android SharedPreferences conventions.
Avare source code (strings.xml) defines `<string name="TrackUp">"Track Up"</string>`, meaning the literal XML key is 'Track Up'.
Avare source code (arrays.xml) defines UnitsDValues, saving '0' to the XML for Nautical Miles, not string text.
Avare source code writes standard Android boolean preferences (value="true").

privileged_info_summary: The data for this task operates on a real open-source aviation app (Avare) running in an Android emulator. A PI audit reveals that the verifier contains several critical flaws that will cause valid solutions to fail. The verifier strictly checks the XML contents for 'TrackUp' (without a space), but Avare saves the 'Map Orientation' preference using the exact string key 'Track Up'. The verifier also incorrectly assumes the 'Distance/Speed Units' preference saves the literal string 'Knot', but Avare instead saves the enum string '0' for Nautical Miles. Finally, the verifier's regex for the 'Draw Tracks' setting (checking for '.*Track.*' set to 'true') erroneously triggers on the 'Track Up' preference instead of matching the actual 'Track History' key ('TrkUpdShowHistory').
